be working now, yeah.
It's funny.
It's like my fifth call of the day, and that's the first time that's happened.
So the weird world of technology we live in.
Yeah, seriously.
But great to meet you.
Excited to chat, and I'm guessing that you maybe met Marcel in person yesterday?

Yeah, we were at the Madrona CEO Summit together, and specifically, he was showing me some of the projects that you guys have been working on for Figma and Lovable, and I think really relevant to what we can do at Plus and wanted to see if that works, if you guys have capacity for us, and all that kind of stuff.
But I can give you a quick background on the company.
I mean...
We can go from there, too.
Yeah, that sounds great.
And I guess, like, what did Marcel tell you around those use cases?
And the reason I ask is because right now, like, with all the demand, we are trying to prioritize, like, our editorial work stream.
And the work that we're doing for, like, Lovable, it's fairly custom, right?
And more of, like, a one-off project.
So Marcel, obviously, is the decision maker there.
But what did he tell you about that use case in particular?
Yeah.
So I'll give the background of the company as well.
I think it just helps.
So basically, what we do is we help people make presentations using AI.

So if you've seen Gamma, it's very similar, except our main thing is that we are focused more on, like, the professional presentation maker, like, people who work inside of PowerPoint or Google Slides.
So if you compare it to, like, the AI coding market, we're trying to be more of, like, a windsurf cursor, where it's, like, for people who are actually...
They like engineers.
For us, it's people who are actually consultants or account execs or whatever who have to live inside of PowerPoint versus like, you know, anyone just wants to stand up a web app.
can probably use like more of like a local or a replet type of thing.
And it's not as much of like the targeting the software engineer.
We do the same thing for presentations.
We help people make native PowerPoint slides, native Google slides.
When you use our tool to generate a presentation, it actually uses like your slide masters, your template.
It's compatible with PowerPoint.
And you don't have to worry about export compatibility issues, you know, all that kind of stuff.
The business.
That's awesome.
I could use that to be honest with you.
We're still like, we're still just creating a hodgepodge of Google slides that could look better.
Totally.
So we can just like automate all of your decks for you.
And, you know, the nice part is when you edit it, it's just like a normal slide deck.
You don't have to like send people a link that says like, hey, I made this deck for you with AI.
And you don't need everyone else at the company to sign up for whatever product that you're using.
So the company's been growing quite well.
So there's actually only four of us full time, but we're about to hit 6 million of ARR.
So, you know, one of these like profitable AI companies, I'd say like we've been smaller than Gamma.
I think they just have like a larger market of people who are like, want to create a wide variety of things, more of like the Canva market.
And now they're doing some website stuff too.
But for us, we're, you know, more focused on people who want to find like the best AI for PowerPoint, the best AI for Google Slides.
So most of our growth to date has come through SEO.
So, you know, largely, I think we'd be interested in all the different types of things that we could do with growthx.
Specifically, the stuff that you guys have been working on with Figma lovable would love to explore if we could do that type of thing.
So the thing that I love to have on our site is basically, you know, a landing page with an example prompt and an example output of every single thing you could make with the tool.

And then, like, a lot of those pages that had already been set up, it's like, we just need that structure and then some of the SEO research to go and specifically find, like, what keywords we should target.
So that's the thing that we've been looking for.
I was telling Marcel just yesterday generally about, like, our SEO strategy and what we've been doing.
And it's like, we've tried a bunch of different things and now it just seems like Google is, like, nerfing us on a bunch of different things.
So we'd love to figure out what are the ways that we can continue investing in SEO profitably and just, like, doing more programmatic stuff as well as possible.
Okay.
Yeah, it makes a lot of sense.
Thanks for giving me all the context and breaking it down.
Who's been creating the content for you guys in terms of the SEO stuff?
Yeah, so for, like, our most important content, like, the best of articles and, like, the...
How to use ChatGPT to create a PowerPoint presentation, like the stuff that converts really well.
I've just been doing it or my co-founder does it.
And then we have a couple of writers who do like the educational content around like, how do I create borders in PowerPoint?
How do I like move slides around?
How do I, you know, that kind of thing.
The big SEO project that we've always wanted to go undertake is more of like the template gallery and like, how do we go and create, you a thousand different pages?
We just haven't had the resources to do it to date.
Okay.
Makes sense.
I think the way that this would probably end up going is we would start with you guys just based on like the capacity and the number of options that we're choosing between.
Like we'd start with you guys on the editorial, but then we would have that programmatic use case in mind during phase two.
And at a certain point after we'd built like a large enough editorial foundation, we could switch gears and start focusing on the programmatic use case without affecting price, right?
Assuming the complexity isn't too crazy if we're doing one work stream at a time.
So I guess the question that I'm asking in a long-winded way, in a longer-rounded way, is the editorial use case important to you?
Because it would need to start there.
If it's like a price thing, I would love to chat further about what we could do to get editorial, I mean, programmatic going right away.
If you guys have good ideas on us for how to do editorial and get it going right away, I'm open to discussing.
I think just for us, there's just not a lot more content that we would be putting out that I think is going to be helpful and would actually convert.
So, for example, we have the best AI tools for this, the best presentation makers, the best blah, blah, blah.
We have the versus articles, we have the alternative stuff.
So a lot of the content that we're doing is kind of just like, it's not like slop that it's...
It's written by AI, but it's just like, we're just churning out random how-to articles because that seems like the thing that's still relevant to our product.
And maybe there's some chance of people looking at it and then being like, oh, what is this?
If you have ideas for like other big buckets and clusters of things to do on the editorial side, happy to consider it.
But I don't know exactly what that would be.
That makes sense.
And maybe I'm just like missing something here, but I guess like, why do I not see any of the types of articles that you're talking about in the organic?
This is the wrong Plus AI.
So do PlusAI.com.
My fault.
Okay.
And I have PlusAI.com up.
So of course.
Of course.

Okay.
Yeah.
You guys do have a ton of different organic.
Yeah.
Y'all have done a great job with this stuff.
Okay, cool.
think.

Click on a domain overview.

And it's just like low-cost, bajillion different templates.
But, you know, there's, like, another example of a competitor that isn't a competitor, but, like, sells the ability to make more interactive slide decks, right?
Yeah.
Okay, cool.
I think I'm going to need, the short of this is I'm going to need to take this, like, use case back to the team.

And with the rest of the time that we have, like, I could either give you more context on, like, growthx, the way we work, if you didn't already get that from Marcel, or we could talk about this programmatic use case more so that I can have the right context to bring to the team.

Thank
Let's do it.

And when you search.

I think for the stuff that Marcel had shown me me.
that

Understood.

I don't think there's too much to be picked up that's already done.

So I think we need some help figuring out, like, how do we get, like, the presentation to turn into something that's actually, like, SEO optimized and searchable?

The right thousand pages, just meaning, like, the right presentations to...
Yeah.
It's like, the the top.

different I'm know...

Okay, so, like, basically different use cases that someone would want to create a presentation around.
That would be what we're trying to programmatically solve for.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
So, connect it back to that Uber example that you gave, like...
So that would be like a separate thing of like, I think, there's like a huge SEO opportunity to go and build a better version of SlideShare because it's just this neglected product.

So if, I don't know, if it would be easier to work on that type of thing for you guys, where it's like, yeah, here's just like a general scaffold framework for how you can go and like put a bunch of presentations into a thing.
And maybe you can just like hand over like the scaffold and we go and figure out how to like fill in the content.
Would also be interested in something like that.
Okay, interesting.
And for like one of these pages, that would be like one of the thousand different presentation pages.
Like walk me through what all would need to be on one of those pages.
Like, you know, today you have like the PowerPoint viewer, you have some like description of what the template is.
Like, help me understand what would be all the different components on that programmatic page that we're creating.
Yeah, I think this is where I would love to lean on you guys for the SEO expertise.
you.
Thank you.

The main things that I would be looking for is just the keyword of one-pager, pitch deck, webinar generator, whatever, the prompt that someone could use to go and generate that content, and then the PowerPoint output of what it looks like so someone can quickly see.

That feels like a pretty light content page to me, so would love to get from you guys and how you think about it for other clients of how do you flesh that out so that it's not like a thin content page that gets penalized by Google or whatever.
I see.
So you're fairly flexible and leaning on us in terms of the expertise of what will be optimized for SEO.
Is there a good example of a page today that we could sort of piggyback off of?
I think this is like the closest that we have, like these things, and then also, we have our...

Yeah, he probably showed you a mock-up that we're working on.
Which is more like this.
We have, like, an example prompt.
Okay, yeah, thanks for bearing with all my questions.

Yeah, I think it's the general, like, SEO stuff, too.
Just, like, why is Google...
Well, That's
Analyzing our site when some of these other, like, super slop sites are continuing to do well.
With the additional ad placement?

Does that work for you?
That works.

Okay.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss potential collaboration between Plus AI and growthx.ai for SEO optimization and content creation.](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Plus AI seeks to improve SEO performance, focusing on programmatic content creation for presentation templates and examples](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1.0)
  - [Current challenges include declining organic traffic and increased ad competition](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=9.0)
  - [growthx.ai to evaluate capacity for both editorial and programmatic content projects, with a preference for programmatic work](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)

## Topics

### Plus AI Overview

  - [AI-powered presentation creation tool focused on professional users (e.g., consultants, account executives)](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=97.0)
  - [Compatible with PowerPoint and Google Slides, using existing templates and slide masters](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=141.0)
  - [4 full-time employees, approaching $6M ARR](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=182.0)
  - [Main growth through SEO, but experiencing recent traffic decline](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=211.0)

### SEO Challenges and Opportunities

  - [Significant drop in organic traffic, especially since June updates](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=266.0)
  - [Increased ad competition for branded searches](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
  - [Two main programmatic SEO opportunities identified:](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=761.0)
    1.  Showcase examples of presentations created with Plus AI
    2.  Build a comprehensive library of presentations (SlideShare-style)

### Proposed Programmatic Content Strategy

  - [Create \~1,000 pages for various presentation use cases (e.g., one-pagers, pitch decks)](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=741.0)
  - [Each page to include:](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1136.0)
      - [Relevant keyword](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
      - [Example prompt for generation](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
      - [PowerPoint output preview](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
      - [SEO-optimized content (to be determined with growthx.ai's expertise)](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1160.0)

### Technical Considerations

  - [Current website built on Webflow, with some programmatic elements on Vercel](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=832.0)
  - [API available for generating presentations](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
  - [PowerPoint viewer implemented for previews](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1231.0)
  - [Challenge: Making presentation content SEO-friendly and searchable](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=999.0)

### Competitive Landscape

  - [Main competitors: Beautiful AI, Gamma](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=571.0)
  - [SEO competition from slide template websites with extensive backlinks](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=582.0)

## Next Steps

  - [growthx.ai to evaluate the programmatic use case with their team](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1174.0)
  - [Assess additional editorial opportunities, if any](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)
  - [Provide clear answer on capacity for programmatic execution (immediate vs. early next year)](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1389.0)
  - [If proceeding, schedule weekly 30-minute meetings with Dan for the first 8 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1432.0)
  - [Dan to provide content support; Plus AI engineering team available for technical assistance](https://fathom.video/share/C1gC3RGCMX6GCNyirZyEu62tvp3JHeSh?tab=summary&timestamp=1426.0)

action_items.description: Present Plus AI use case to growthx team. Evaluate programmatic/editorial opportunities. Prepare clear answer on editorial potential + programmatic execution timeline (current capacity vs. early next year). Report back to Dan Li within 1 week.
Be prepared for potential 30-min weekly meetings w/ growthx team for first 8 weeks. Standby to provide content assistance + engineering support as needed.
